mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals broad economic expansion, raising corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change vs subsequent 60-day SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period, or negative returns occur in more than 50% of observations when PAYEMS growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 8.0% indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses below 8.0%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is negative or not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) across all post-2000 instances.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production and retail sales exhibit positive month-over-month growth, the economy is expanding on both supply and demand sides, reinforcing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: In months when both series are positive, the 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 30% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25%, the central bank has reached the zero lower bound and is likely to deploy unconventional easing (e.g., quantitative easing) which supports asset prices and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the mean 20-day S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) or negative returns occur in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 800 thousand units signals a recovery in the housing market, which stimulates construction activity, related industries, and consumer wealth, lifting equity markets over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: After HOUST crosses above 800k, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is negative or not significantly positive across all historical occurrences since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates (month-over-month > 0.2%) while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, real interest rates turn deeply negative, incentivizing a shift from cash to equities and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over a short horizon.
regime_hint: low interest rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: In periods when both conditions hold, the 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index signals eroding purchasing power, which prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is positive, the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, contradicting the negative direction prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 9%, the economy is in a distressed state that triggers strong expectations of fiscal and monetary stimulus, which in turn supports risk assets and produces a positive short-term S&P 500 return over the coming week.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE above 9%, the average 5-day S&P 500 return is negative or not significantly positive across all post-1960 events, or the win rate is below 50%.
